_schema: feature-name -> { chain, txid, date }. A catalogue feature is 'verified' ONLY when a real, explorer-confirmed testnet or mainnet txid exists here. This file is the SOLE source of the 'verified' state; the catalogue reads it and nothing else writes it. Code inspection, passing tests, and clean review NEVER promote a feature to verified (see CLAUDE.md 'Verify, don't assert'). Empty = nothing on-chain-verified yet; every code-complete feature shows 'built' until a txid lands here.

_corrected.USDT mainnet send (full UI path, build:r: Txid corrected 2026-06-22. PR #280 originally recorded 0x3f2fe19a1092af36b4ca758846c4d266fd55f947efc110a1122013c2b242b986 — that hash is a real mainnet tx but targets the USDC contract, not USDT, so it did not substantiate the USDT claim. The actual USDT mainnet send is 0xf06a0ba731d1b8bf4d3f859a5904830b2f064725ba837c8c7332e5264f0b5b08 (verified on-chain via eth_getTransactionByHash/Receipt 2026-06-22: to=Tether USDT contract 0xdAC17F95…831ec7, transfer 1 USDT to 0x82D0…5BAB, chainId 1, status SUCCESS, block 25360159). Entry below now carries the correct txid.

_h1_passkey_2fa_fix_confirmation._why_not_in_evidence: Deliberately a META key, NOT under 'evidence' — so it does NOT promote any catalogue feature to 'verified'. The Two-Factor feature stays BUILT. This records that the ECC audit H-1 fix (passkey-2FA was silently bypassed on Send) was exercised end-to-end on-chain, while being honest that the authenticator was SOFTWARE, not a Secure Enclave.
_h1_passkey_2fa_fix_confirmation.finding: ECC audit H-1 — SendCrypto keyed the 2FA gate on actionPasswordConfigured alone, silently bypassing a passkey-only second factor. Fixed in PR #340 (resolveSend2faMethod).
_h1_passkey_2fa_fix_confirmation.what_was_verified: Fully-automated web e2e (Playwright + Chrome CDP virtual authenticator). Imported the real throwaway testnet seed (NOT demo), registered a passkey, enabled PIN+Passkey 2FA, and drove a real Sepolia ETH send through the full UI. The send screen rendered the TwoFactorGate ('Authorise this send with your PIN + passkey') — i.e. the passkey factor was ENFORCED, not bypassed — and broadcast ONLY after a genuine WebAuthn assertion (CDP credentialAsserted event; authenticator signCount 1->2).
_h1_passkey_2fa_fix_confirmation.verified_onchain: eth_getTransactionByHash + eth_getTransactionReceipt, chainId 11155111, status SUCCESS, gasUsed 21000, 2026-06-23
_h1_passkey_2fa_fix_confirmation.caveats: (1) The authenticator is a CDP virtual (software) authenticator, NOT a Secure Enclave — this confirms the H-1 WIRING (no silent bypass; the assertion genuinely gates the send), which is what H-1 is, but NOT the hardware-possession strength of the factor. A physical-device (iPhone Face ID / security key) passkey send is still the bar to flip Two-Factor from BUILT to 'verified'. (2) navigator.webdriver was masked to false so RASP did not block the test harness — touches RASP's automation probe only, never the 2FA path. Incidentally, with webdriver=true RASP DID block the send (blockedByRisk suppressed the gate), confirming RASP is wired into SendCrypto.

USDT mainnet send (full UI path, build:r.note: Corrects the wrong txid (0x3f2fe19a…, a USDC tx) recorded in PR #280 — see _corrected.

dApp Connector — eth_sendTransaction (D3.path: D3 — handleSendTransaction code path (CAIP-2 parse → getNetworkByChainId → VULN-19 chain-ID guard → 1M gas cap → ethers.Wallet.sendTransaction → broadcast)
